Position Overview
Mechanical Engineering
Provide expert opinion, guidance, approval and leadership on mechanical, structural and metallurgical designs to engineering and operations to ensure the company maintains compliance to the EU Pressure Equipment Directive, UK Pressure Equipment (Safety) Regulation and Pressure systems Safety Regulations. Provide leadership on mechanical integrity to ensure risk of mechanical failures of critical equipment are mitigated.
Project Engineering
Provide Project management services for implementation of assigned projects. Lead, define, plan & execute key capital projects in conjunction with site teams and Europe engineering and technical resources.
Job Responsibilities

Lead the management of Mechanical Integrity across all European sites, overseeing integrity assessments for high risk assets, responding to outcomes of inspections and management of changes through internal and external resources.
Ownership of the Mechanical Integrity Criticality Assessment Register, revising and updated based on findings from integrity assessment and the addition of new assets from projects.
Working with external consultants to develop RBI based written schemes of examination for all mechanical integrity assets (pressure vessels, heat exchanges, tanks, pipework, etc). Establishing scope, extent and amount levels.
SME for asset Mechanical Integrity throughout the business with regards to assessing degradation mechanisms, modes of failure, identification of materials for hazardous applications, developing repair plans and investigations into failures/degradation, in conjunction with the external experts (like an Axiom).
Performance reporting of asset condition and compliance of the MI Inspections with the use of SAP. Understanding high risk assets, end of life planning requirements and where assets may be non-compliant with overdue integrity assessment.
Generating design scopes of works for all aspects of mechanical (eg piping & vessels) and structural application for projects across Europe.
Approval authority for all mechanical and structural designs for all EU sites.
Developing and owning mechanical and structural engineering standards (eg pipe service index, pipe classes, pipe stress, vessel design, metallurgy selection, structural design, etc).
Manage the capital plan and projects for the site, working with the EU Projects and engineering team and the EU Automation team to deliver design content suitable for project execution on time and in budget for the site.
The role-holder has jurisdiction to make decisions relating to: -

The postholder collaborates with stakeholders (site maintenance leaders, site leaders, central and site engineering resources) in the decision-making process to mutually agree key decisions e.g. repair plans from mechanical integrity inspection findings.
Final approval of piping and vessel inspection protocols typically knows as Written Schemes of Examination (WSE) and Risk Based Inspection (RBI) plans for all EU sites
Final approval of mechanical equipment repair plans across all EU sites
Final approval of all mechanical and structural designs for new or modified equipment and across all EU sites.
Functional Competencies
Future Focus

Able to effectively provide guidance to the operations organization, supporting the strategies, goals, and objectives of the business.
Trust & Teamwork

Able to effectively build, lead and participate in teams.
Able to effectively communicate, both verbally and written.
Personal Leadership

Exhibit drive and influencing skills to arrive at solutions.
Leverage other Bakelite Synthetics sites, processes, and designs to address issues to keep from “reinventing the wheel” – take a global perspective.
Demonstrate a strong sense of integrity and courage to make decisions and outcomes in Bakelite Synthetics best interest.
Achieves Business Results

Able to develop plans on both short-term and multiyear horizons.
Involving key stakeholders in activities.
Education & Experience
Required

University degree in an Mechanical engineering.
Knowledge of applicable EU standards and Directives related to mechanical and structural design and operation. Examples include Pressure Equipment Directive 2014/68/EU, Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC and Seveso Directive.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, including effective communication with others for whom English is their second language.
Experience in a Mechanical or Project Engineering role within the chemical industry or other process industries, with minimum 10 years’ experience.
Managing Mechanical Integrity programmes, form WSE through to Inspection, degradation analysis and repairs.
Travel for periods within Europe (typical duration 1 week). Travel when business requires.

About the Role

We are looking for a Product Development Engineer to join our Product team and contribute to the evolution of bound4blue’s eSAIL technology from an engineering perspective.

This role focuses on developing, evaluating, and validating new mechanical and structural concepts for our product. You will be responsible for exploring innovative solutions, assessing their technical feasibility, and supporting the transition of selected developments from concept and prototype stages into validated product improvements.

Working closely with the Product Director and cross-functional engineering teams, you will play a key role in strengthening the technical robustness, performance, and manufacturability of our eSAIL systems.


Responsibilities


Contribute to the definition and maintenance of the Product Technology Roadmap in close alignment with the Product Director, ensuring consistency with long-term product strategy and objectives.

Lead and support technology development initiatives aimed at improving key product attributes such as structural performance, cost-efficiency, weight reduction, reliability, repairability, and manufacturability.

Perform mechanical and structural analysis, calculations, and design studies to evaluate new concepts, materials, components, and design solutions.

Propose, design, and support testing activities (laboratory, prototype, or field testing) to validate new technical solutions and assess their feasibility for product integration.

Create, review, and maintain assigned technical documentation (e.g. ETS, INS, ETD), ensuring technical accuracy, completeness, and consistency, as delegated by the Product Director.

Collaborate with suppliers of critical technologies or components, supporting technical evaluation and knowledge transfer activities.

Support the handover of validated technologies and solutions to the Operations team prior to standard product implementation.

Promote effective communication and technical alignment with cross-functional teams, including Operations, Automation & Control, Aerodynamics, Naval Architecture, and Marketing.

Contribute to the continuous improvement of product development processes, technical standards, and engineering best practices.


Requirements


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Naval Engineering, Structural Engineering, or a related field.

+6 years of solid experience in mechanical and/or structural engineering roles, ideally related to product development, R&D, or advanced engineering.

Strong background in structural analysis, mechanical design, and engineering calculations.

Hands-on experience with FEM tools such as ANSYS or equivalent structural analysis software.

Experience with 3D CAD tools (e.g. SolidWorks or similar).

Experience in the naval industry is highly valued; experience in related industries such as wind energy, offshore, or heavy industrial equipment is also relevant.

Analytical mindset, curiosity, and a strong problem-solving orientation.

Ability to work collaboratively in cross-functional engineering environments.

Professional level of Spanish and English.


What Awaits You at bound4blue


A hands-on role at the core of product innovation and engineering development.

The opportunity to influence the technical evolution of a unique, high-impact product.

Collaboration with multidisciplinary engineering teams in a cutting-edge clean-tech environment.

A scale-up context where ideas are tested, decisions are close to the engineering work, and technical contributions are highly visible.

Sobre PLD Space

PLD Space es una compañía internacional de servicios espaciales con ingeniería y fabricación propia. Se dedica al transporte al espacio de satélites y personas, integrando verticalmente ingeniería, ensayos, fabricación y operaciones de sus cohetes reutilizables y sostenibles. Con sede central en Elche (España) y fundada en 2011 por Raúl Torres y Raúl Verdú, PLD Space se ha consolidado como un referente en el sector de lanzadores espaciales a nivel global. Su familia de lanzadores MIURA y su cápsula tripulada LINCE posicionan a la empresa como líder de la soberanía tecnológica europea en el transporte espacial, cubriendo todo el rango de misiones espaciales.

Con un equipo de más de 400 empleados y 220 millones de financiación total, PLD Space cuenta con el mayor banco de pruebas privado de Europa, situado en Teruel (España), y opera una base de lanzamiento en el Centro Espacial Guayanés (CSG), en Kourou (Guayana Francesa). En total, dispone de más de 188.000m2 de infraestructuras industriales bajo gestión.

El trabajo en equipo, el coraje, la determinación y la ambición permiten a PLD Space garantizar una capacidad autónoma e independiente de acceso al espacio para satélites basada en la fiabilidad a un precio competitivo a nivel global. La compañía es capaz de ofrecer los mejores servicios de lanzamiento a operadores comerciales de satélites, instituciones, agencias espaciales y entidades gubernamentales nacionales e internacionales.

Responsabilidades

Realizar análisis de cargas térmicas y mecánicas para componentes del motor.
Llevar a cabo análisis e interpretación de datos de pruebas de propulsión y de vuelo.
Generar documentación de ingeniería.
Mantenimiento y mejora los enfoques estándar del programa para el análisis.

About PLD

PLD Space is an international space services company with in-house engineering and manufacturing. It is dedicated to transporting satellites and humans into space, vertically integrating engineering, testing, manufacturing and operations of its reusable and sustainable rockets. Headquartered in Elche (Spain) and founded in 2011 by Raúl Torres and Raúl Verdú, PLD Space has established itself as a global benchmark in the space launcher sector. Its MIURA family of launchers and its LINCE manned capsule position the company as a leader in European technological sovereignty in space transportation, covering the full range of space missions.

With a team of more than 400 employees and 220 million in total funding, PLD Space also has the largest private test benches in Europe, located in Teruel (Spain), and operates a launch base in the Guiana Space Centre (CSG), in Kourou (French Guiana). In total, it has more than 188,000m2 of industrial infrastructures under management.

Teamwork, courage, determination and ambition enable PLD Space to guarantee an autonomous, independent satellite space access capability, based on reliability at a globally competitive price. The company is able to offer best-in-class launch services to commercial satellite operators, institutions, space agencies and national and international governmental entities.

Mission

As Avionics AIT Engineer at PLD Space, your main mission is to:

Plan, define, control and manage the AIT activities of avionics equipment at unit level.
Plan, define and support the AIT activities of avionics equipment at both subsystem (major assemblies) and system levels.
Define and/or optimize the processes for the unit-level assembly and integration of avionics equipment.

Responsibilities

Read, interpret, and analyze avionics requirements to derive test specifications.
Perform tailoring of normative, standards and recommendations applicable to the assembly, integration, and testing activities.
Define assembly, integration and test plans and procedures of avionics equipment at unit level.
Define test plans and procedures for subsystem-level integrated assemblies (Avionics Test Bench in Hardware-in-the-Loop).
Support the execution of unit test campaigns (design verification, qualification and acceptance).
Support and audit the correct integration and functional validation of avionics equipment installed in major assemblies, which may be used for both testing and flight (engines, turbo-pumps, tanks, vehicle sections and stages, etc.).
Generate and release on schedule engineering documentation such as Test Specifications, AIT Plans, AIT Procedures, and Verification Control Documents.
Document the verification and validation results thoroughly and clearly via review meetings.
Partner with production engineers to implement work instructions and integration procedures of avionics equipment in major assemblies.
Work closely with and consistently provide feedback to responsible design engineers, manufacturing engineers, and test lab teams to drive design or infrastructure improvements to increase product reliability and yield.

Necessary Requirements

University Degree - Engineer (Electronic, Electrical, Aerospace, Telecommunications, or equivalent)
3+ years of experience with avionics AIT activities.
2+ years of experience in a hardware and software testing environment.
Work independently, in a structured and results-oriented manner to achieve goals on time and to coordinate work processes efficiently.
Strong communication skills.
Language - English and Spanish.

Desired Competencies

Knowledge of ECSS, IPC, NASA, and MIL Standards.
Experience with assembly, integration and testing of avionics hardware such as flight computers, sensors, batteries, power control and distribution units, antennas, transmitters, receivers, electrical and electro-mechanical actuators, etc.
Experience with environmental testing such as thermal cycling, thermal vacuum, vibration, shock, EMI/EMC.
Understanding of electrical schematics and electronics manufacturing to analyze and improve test coverage.
Experience analyzing circuits and developing functional test plans.
Proven track record solving complex problems with minimal supervision and on a tight schedule.
Experience in working in clean room and EPA (Electrostatic Protection Area) environments.
Previous experience in Launch Vehicles projects.
